input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't know about .NET 10 at all. Let me try to use a newer version of BenchmarkDotNet, or create the CoreRuntime manually. 

Actually, CoreRuntime has a `CreateForNewVersion` or a constructor that takes a moniker and tfm string. Let me check...

Actually, `CoreRuntime` has a static method `CreateForNewVersion(string monikerString, string tfm)` or we can do `new CoreRuntime(RuntimeMoniker moniker, string msBuildMoniker, string displayName)`.

But the RuntimeMoniker enum doesn't have .NET 10. Let me try to use a different approach.


I could try using `CoreRuntime.CreateForNewVersion` with "net10.0" and ".NET 10.0" as parameters, or I could upgrade to a newer BenchmarkDotNet version that might already support .NET 10. Let me start with the `CreateForNewVersion` approach first.
